sources.label: Simply Wall St - Azbil FY2026 results: margin decline vs growth story
BigGo Finance - Azbil ToSTNeT-3 treasury share repurchase notice (May 12, 2026)
Memoori - Azbil AI & data center strategy (3 key strategic moves)

verdicts.rankRationale: No material news since Jun 6; last datapoints remain the May 13 FY results (Q4 EPS beat 52%, Building Automation-led, FY2026 guide of Y315B revenue) and a mid-May buyback. Today's -1.9% drift with the thesis intact marginally improves entry asymmetry but is not enough to re-rank a name yesterday's agent already held.
verdicts.bull: Building Automation is Azbil's largest segment and the company is explicitly pushing AI/data-center cooling and airflow-management solutions (Chiller Plant Digital Twin, DATAFLUCT/X1Studio investments) while the market still prices it as a sleepy Japanese controls company at ~$5B cap, +8.7% YTD - latent, under-owned exposure intact.
verdicts.neutral: FY2026 guidance of +5.4% revenue and 2.4% consensus annual growth say the data-center optionality is not yet showing up in numbers; until DC-specific order disclosure appears, the stock trades on Japanese retrofit cycle dynamics.
verdicts.bear: Net margin slipped to 12.9% from 13.6%, and reliance on Japanese retrofit projects plus a plateau in domestic new-office development could cap Building Automation growth before the data-center narrative ever monetizes.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Disclosure of data-center-specific order intake or a hyperscaler/colocation BMS win in Japan's DC buildout, plus Q1 FY2026 results showing Building Automation order momentum.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Japan building retrofit cycle rolls over while data-center revenue stays too small to disclose, leaving a low-growth controls company with eroding margins.
